setting.note: The dusty plain of La Mancha, its inns and windmills, and the long road east that finally reaches Barcelona. Cervantes maps his knight onto real ground: the Campo de Montiel he sets out across, the windmills traditionally those of Campo de Criptana, El Toboso where Dulcinea is supposed to live, the Sierra Morena where he does penance, and the Barcelona beach where he is beaten. The places are real; the giants are windmills.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Miguel de Cervantes, first published 1605-1615; the author died in 1616.
rights.translation: English translation by John Ormsby (1885); he died in 1895, so the translation is public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU: Cervantes died 1616, Ormsby 1895.
rights.attribution: Miguel de Cervantes, Don Quixote (1605), trans. John Ormsby. Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The gold of the imagined golden age of chivalry that Don Quixote is forever trying to restore. A deep gold that clears WCAG AA on paper.

quixote.name: Don Quixote
quixote.colorSourceQuote: mantle of the finest scarlet cloth
